jokes.text: Right, right, so you went on a group date with a bunch of ugly chicks to make yourself look good, and you tricked a guy.
jokes.setup: Mac processes how Dee could get a boyfriend
jokes.punchline: His immediate assumption that she had to use deception and ugly friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - cynical and cruel but delivered matter-of-factly. Shows his inability to believe Dee could attract someone honestly.

jokes.text: Yes, of course, that's exactly what I did, and it worked well, and-and now we're going steady.
jokes.setup: Mac's accusation about her deceptive dating tactics
jokes.punchline: Dee proudly confirms the accusation and uses antiquated dating terminology
jokes.explanation: Dee's complete lack of shame about manipulation plus the dated 'going steady' language creates cringe comedy.

jokes.text: The next thing you know, it was all asses and hands and tits and... I could slip right in and out without anybody noticing. It was great.
jokes.setup: Frank reminiscing about '70s group dating
jokes.punchline: His graphic description of orgies followed by casual admission of his sneaky participation
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank - inappropriately sexual with perfect character voice. The 'slip in and out' double entendre works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: I don't know if you guys noticed this, but, like, I'm kind of having a hard time closing the deal with the waitress.
jokes.setup: Charlie seeks dating advice
jokes.punchline: His massive understatement about his obvious stalker-like obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie delusion. The phrase 'kind of having a hard time' for his years of stalking is brilliantly understated.

jokes.text: Is he...? Yeah, we've noticed.
jokes.setup: Charlie's oblivious question about whether they noticed his waitress problem
jokes.punchline: The immediate, weary confirmation from both guys
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat. The overlapping response shows how obvious Charlie's problem is to everyone.

jokes.text: As an erotic man, I have been keeping my eye on this buncher situation
jokes.setup: Dennis positioning himself as the dating expert
jokes.punchline: His pretentious self-description as 'an erotic man'
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis narcissism. 'Erotic man' is such a ridiculous self-designation that perfectly captures his delusional self-image.

jokes.text: All right, here's to orgies.
jokes.setup: The gang planning their group dating strategy
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual toast treating group dates as orgies
jokes.explanation: Frank's perverted worldview in four words. The casual toast format makes it funnier than a regular statement.

jokes.text: Man, I'm really nervous. I'm, like, sweating through my pants.
jokes.setup: Pre-date nerves
jokes.punchline: The specific, gross detail of sweating through pants
jokes.explanation: Typical Charlie grossness. The specificity of sweating through pants rather than just sweating adds to the cringe.

jokes.text: Have you brought cheese? Yeah, you need some?
jokes.setup: Charlie's nervous sweating
jokes.punchline: Mac casually offering cheese as a solution to anxiety
jokes.explanation: Completely absurd but delivered naturally. Mac carrying cheese and treating it as normal anxiety relief is perfect Always Sunny logic.

jokes.text: See, the girls will see the leash, and I'll say, 'I've got a rescue dog outside,' thereby, 'D,' demonstrating my value as a compassionate man.
jokes.setup: Mac asking about Dennis's dog leash
jokes.punchline: Dennis's elaborate, calculated manipulation scheme disguised as compassion
jokes.explanation: Introduces the DENNIS system perfectly - his sociopathic planning presented as dating advice. The clinical breakdown is chilling and funny.

jokes.text: Later, when we go outside, the dog will be missing. I'll become hysterical and suggest that, maybe, the dog ran back to the apartment.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his fake rescue dog plan
jokes.punchline: The elaborate fake emotional manipulation escalating to apartment seduction
jokes.explanation: The escalation from fake compassion to calculated emotional manipulation is perfectly Dennis. The clinical planning of fake hysteria is darkly brilliant.

jokes.text: He let it go. It probably got obliterated by a taxi or something.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking what happened to the dog
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual assumption that Dennis actually killed a dog
jokes.explanation: Mac's matter-of-fact assumption that Dennis would actually abandon a dog to traffic shows how well he knows Dennis's sociopathy.

jokes.text: There never was a dog. Oh, my God! Wait, wait, wait, wait. There was no dog?
jokes.setup: Confusion about the fake dog plan
jokes.punchline: Charlie's delayed, horrified realization followed by more confusion
jokes.explanation: Charlie's innocent confusion about the fake dog creates a nice contrast to Dennis's calculated sociopathy.

jokes.text: I thought we got a dog.
jokes.setup: The confusion about Dennis's fake dog plan
jokes.punchline: Mac's simple confusion about whether they actually acquired a dog
jokes.explanation: Mac's innocent confusion adds to the chaos of Dennis's over-complicated plan. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: I just dropped a whole bunch of money on a new promise ring for my man.
jokes.setup: Dee talking about making money
jokes.punchline: Her casual mention of buying a promise ring for her new boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Sets up Dee's desperate over-investment in a casual relationship. The promise ring detail shows her delusion.

jokes.text: Corral him, because God knows he'll never ask you.
jokes.setup: Frank thinking Dee is proposing
jokes.punchline: His brutal assessment that no man would propose to Dee
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank brutality delivered as practical advice. 'Corral him' treats dating like livestock management.

jokes.text: You sound like a 12-year-old. It's a total turn-off. My cock ring is sliding off.
jokes.setup: Frank criticizing Dee's relationship language
jokes.punchline: His complaint escalating to his cock ring falling off from lack of arousal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from dating criticism to sexual dysfunction. Frank connecting Dee's childish language to his erectile issues is perfectly twisted.

jokes.text: Why my father is talking to me with a cinch around his penis is beyond my grasp.
jokes.setup: Frank's cock ring comment
jokes.punchline: Dee's uncomfortable acknowledgment of the inappropriate family dynamic
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat. Dee's clinical description 'cinch around his penis' shows her attempt to process Frank's inappropriateness.

jokes.text: Oh, shit. It fell off. Help me find it.
jokes.setup: Frank's cock ring sliding off
jokes.punchline: The immediate practical problem and his request for help
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to the cock ring gag. Frank asking for help finding it adds to the inappropriate family dynamic.

jokes.text: I tell you, when I snuggle up next to him on the couch, I'd swear that... that he's the one who rescued me.
jokes.setup: Dennis continuing his fake dog story
jokes.punchline: His calculated emotional vulnerability about being 'rescued' by a dog
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of Dennis's manipulation. The 'rescued me' reversal is a classic manipulative technique delivered with fake sincerity.

jokes.text: The waitress got bit by a chow once, and I kicked the shit out of the thing. You kicked a dog?
jokes.setup: Discussion about dogs
jokes.punchline: Charlie's casual admission of animal abuse followed by the women's horror
jokes.explanation: Charlie's innocent delivery of violent behavior creates perfect cringe. His protective instinct expressed through animal abuse is classically Charlie.

jokes.text: She's the woman I'm in love with, you know? And I follow her around a lot, and she doesn't really give me the time of day
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining about the waitress
jokes.punchline: His casual admission of stalking behavior presented as romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie delusion. 'I follow her around a lot' presented as normal dating behavior is brilliantly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: She's not beautiful. And everybody's banged her. Except for me.
jokes.setup: Charlie calling the waitress beautiful
jokes.punchline: Mac's brutal contradiction followed by Charlie's dejected confirmation
jokes.explanation: Devastating putdown delivered matter-of-factly. The 'except for me' adds perfect pathos to Charlie's situation.

jokes.text: I will strike you down! You're gonna strike me down?! That's our thing. They're doing a bit.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis getting into an argument
jokes.punchline: Their escalating threats followed by the explanation it's a rehearsed bit
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment. The idea that their arguments are rehearsed 'bits' adds another layer to their dysfunction.

jokes.text: While I don't particularly find you conventionally attractive, I do find you oddly sexy.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to flirt during seat switching
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment that insults and compliments simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis negging. The elaborate insult disguised as a compliment shows his manipulative nature and social incompetence.

jokes.text: Can I get a drink? Y... go ask one of the other ones. What other ones? The other ones, the bird lady, the-the troll man.
jokes.setup: Woman asking for drink service
jokes.punchline: Dennis's casual description of his friends as 'bird lady' and 'troll man'
jokes.explanation: Dennis casually dehumanizing his friends while trying to impress women. 'Bird lady' and 'troll man' are perfectly dismissive.

jokes.text: You're moving closer to the men who eat trash and bang transsexuals?
jokes.setup: Woman moving away from Dennis
jokes.punchline: His description of Mac and Charlie as trash-eating transsexual-bangers
jokes.explanation: Dennis's desperate attempt to make himself look better by throwing his friends under the bus. The specific insults are perfectly Dennis.

jokes.text: I'm actually on a Buncher. Wait, what? Yeah, they should be here any minute.
jokes.setup: Dee surprising Ryan at work
jokes.punchline: Ryan revealing he's on a group date while she's giving him a promise ring
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony. Dee's romantic gesture immediately undercut by Ryan's casual infidelity creates peak cringe.

jokes.text: Oh. It's kind of small, huh? Well, it's my dad's.
jokes.setup: Ryan commenting on the cock ring
jokes.punchline: Dee's explanation that it belongs to her father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant payoff to the cock ring setup. Dee casually explaining it's her father's adds layers of inappropriateness.

jokes.text: I'm breaking up with you. Right. Well, I didn't know that we were together.
jokes.setup: The awkward cock ring conversation
jokes.punchline: Ryan's casual revelation that he never considered them together
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Dee's romantic delusion. Ryan's matter-of-fact dismissal destroys her entire narrative.

jokes.text: We killed it, boys. We killed it. This was a great idea. Yeah, but the date sucked.
jokes.setup: Post-date assessment
jokes.punchline: Dennis's delusional confidence contrasted with Mac's honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Dennis's delusion and Mac's realism. Shows Dennis's inability to read social situations.

jokes.text: It did suck for you guys, though. Yeah, I imagine those girls are probably at their apartments right now, trying to figure out how to get in touch with me.
jokes.setup: Dennis acknowledging the date went badly for others
jokes.punchline: His complete delusion about the women wanting to contact him
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis narcissism. His complete misreading of the situation while maintaining supreme confidence is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: One star? Out of five? No, no, no. Me and Charlie got two stars! Oh! Nice! We're better than you, dude!
jokes.setup: Checking their ratings
jokes.punchline: Mac celebrating his marginally better terrible rating
jokes.explanation: Mac's pride in his barely-better awful rating shows the competitive dysfunction. The celebration of 2 out of 5 stars is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: I was a five-star man before the Internet, and I'm damn sure a five-star man now, okay? I just gotta drop the dead weight so I can shine.
jokes.setup: Dennis's poor rating
jokes.punchline: His delusional self-assessment and plan to abandon his friends
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis delusion. 'Five-star man before the Internet' is a perfect encapsulation of his inability to accept criticism.

jokes.text: Ah, this poor jerk has no idea he's about to get played like a goddamn guitar.
jokes.setup: Dennis spotting his next target
jokes.punchline: His overconfident assessment after his recent failures
jokes.explanation: Dennis's unwarranted confidence after complete failure. The 'played like a guitar' metaphor is perfectly Dennis-level pretentious.

jokes.text: The dirty one or the gay one.
jokes.setup: Directing someone to ask other staff for drinks
jokes.punchline: His casual, derogatory descriptions of Mac and Charlie
jokes.explanation: Another example of Dennis casually dehumanizing his friends. Consistent with his character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Creatine shits.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie planning conversation topics
jokes.punchline: Mac's first suggestion being about supplement-induced diarrhea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac topic choice. His fitness obsession leading to discussions of bathroom problems is classically inappropriate.

jokes.text: It's a love story for the ages.
jokes.setup: Discussion about avoiding the waitress topic
jokes.punchline: Charlie's grandiose description of his stalking as epic romance
jokes.explanation: Charlie's delusion about his one-sided obsession being a 'love story for the ages' is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Also, who's going to say grace? We should figure that out now.
jokes.setup: Planning the date conversation
jokes.punchline: Mac's sudden concern about saying grace at a bar date
jokes.explanation: Mac's religious concerns at inappropriate times is perfect character comedy. Planning grace for a bar pickup is absurd.

jokes.text: Oh, what, this? Henry. Henry the dog. He's lost. Probably dead.
jokes.setup: Dennis showing the dog leash
jokes.punchline: His casual assumption that his 'lost' dog is probably dead
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of Dennis's failing manipulation. His immediate jump to 'probably dead' shows his sociopathic calculation.

jokes.text: Look, let's cut to the chase. I like you. I like you very much. This is gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to seduce the doctor
jokes.punchline: His presumptuous announcement that sex is inevitable
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis arrogance. The declarative 'This is gonna happen' shows his complete misunderstanding of consent and social interaction.

jokes.text: If it were a one out of five thing, and it is, what would you give me? The obvious five-star rating, or would you be a liar?
jokes.setup: Dennis obsessing over ratings
jokes.punchline: His leading question that assumes he deserves five stars
jokes.explanation: Dennis's manipulative questioning perfectly shows his desperation for validation. The 'or would you be a liar' ultimatum is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Oh, I have a rescue dog outside. Okay. That was jarring. There's no context for that.
jokes.setup: Dennis randomly bringing up his dog story
jokes.punchline: His self-aware acknowledgment that it came out of nowhere
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware moment. Dennis recognizing his own manipulation attempt failed shows his system breaking down.

jokes.text: Looks like the spider caught herself a fly.
jokes.setup: Dee watching her dating success
jokes.punchline: Her pretentious metaphor about her supposed dating prowess
jokes.explanation: Typical Dee pretension. The spider/fly metaphor shows her inflated self-image about her dating abilities.

jokes.text: I got a great one about a dago fisherman. Oh, no, no, no, no, no. Ethnic jokes are really dicey.
jokes.setup: Frank suggesting a joke
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately recognizing his own suggestion is inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Frank showing rare self-awareness about his inappropriate instincts. The quick self-correction is mildly funny.

jokes.text: cowboys hate teachers. You know, cowboys are independent thinkers who like to learn for themselves.
jokes.setup: Frank suggesting a cowboy and teacher joke
jokes.punchline: Charlie's elaborate theory about cowboy-teacher relations
jokes.explanation: Charlie's random expertise on cowboy philosophy is perfectly absurd. His confident analysis of fictional character conflicts is classic Charlie.

jokes.text: Hi, ladies. I'm Frack. Shit.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie practicing their introductions
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately botching his fake name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective physical comedy. Mac's immediate failure at the simplest task shows his incompetence.

jokes.text: Now I'm a half a star? Half a star? How does a five-star man get...
jokes.setup: Dennis checking his rating again
jokes.punchline: His rating has somehow gotten even worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Dennis's rating crisis. The idea that he's gotten even worse shows his downward spiral.

jokes.text: Call you one star, bitch. Ha!
jokes.setup: Dennis leaving his one-night stand
jokes.punchline: His vindictive rating revenge delivered like a catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Dennis's petty revenge through ratings shows his complete lack of emotional maturity. The triumphant delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: The DEE system. Do them. Establish low rating. Increase power.
jokes.setup: Dee claiming to have her own system
jokes.punchline: Her badly constructed acronym that doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Dee's pathetic attempt to copy Dennis's system shows her competitive nature and lack of originality.

jokes.text: She orders a steak well-done and then complains when the damn thing comes out and it's too dry. Well, it's like, 'Hey, dum-dum, you ordered the damn thing well-done. That's why it's dry, you stupid idiot.'
jokes.setup: Mac practicing conversation about rating quality
jokes.punchline: His elaborate, hostile analogy about steak preparation
jokes.explanation: Mac's aggressive analogy shows his dating incompetence. The hostility toward hypothetical bad reviewers reveals his insecurity.

jokes.text: I like to think of us as mercenaries, a strike force actually. Frank's management, I'm security, Charlie bashes rats.
jokes.setup: Mac describing their bar ownership
jokes.punchline: His grandiose military description of their basic bar jobs
jokes.explanation: Mac's inflated self-image turning bartending into military operations. The progression from mercenaries to rat-bashing is perfect.

jokes.text: I don't really bash them so much as I start to drown them these days so I don't have to listen to their screams.
jokes.setup: Mac describing Charlie as rat basher
jokes.punchline: Charlie's matter-of-fact description of his evolved torture methods
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Charlie moment. His casual evolution to drowning rats to avoid the screams is chillingly funny.

jokes.text: I have 80 of them that live in my alleyway. Too many cats, sounds strange. I have eight cats that sleep in my sink. Still too many.
jokes.setup: Charlie trying to impress women with cat stories
jokes.punchline: His escalating admissions of inappropriate cat hoarding
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Charlie's inability to read social cues. The specific detail about cats sleeping in his sink adds to the grossness.

jokes.text: I'm a five-star man! I'm a five-star man!
jokes.setup: Dennis having a complete meltdown
jokes.punchline: His repeated mantra-like assertion of his five-star status
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis breakdown. The desperate repetition shows his complete psychological collapse over external validation.

jokes.text: But now I know what it feels like to be completely unwanted like you.
jokes.setup: Dennis's apparent apology to the waitress
jokes.punchline: His backhanded empathy that insults her while claiming understanding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis manipulation disguised as empathy. The casual cruelty of 'completely unwanted like you' shows his inability to be genuinely kind.

jokes.text: You 'don't have online'? What the hell does that...? Just... you can just go on your phone and do it on your phone. My phone is, it's so old!
jokes.setup: Waitress saying she doesn't have online
jokes.punchline: Dennis's mounting frustration with her technological limitations
jokes.explanation: Dennis's plans crumbling due to basic technological barriers. His increasing desperation as he realizes she can't help him is perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! That phone is preposterous! How long have you had that? Nine... 90...
jokes.setup: Waitress showing her old phone
jokes.punchline: Dennis's horror and the waitress's inability to remember how long she's had it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dennis's frustration. The waitress's confused counting adds to the digital divide comedy.

jokes.text: I don't need your approval. Goddamn. I don't need anyone's approval. This is stupid. What are you doing? You rating me right now? Well, guess what. Go ahead, rate me! 'Cause I'll rate you, huh?!
jokes.setup: Dennis's complete breakdown
jokes.punchline: His contradictory claims about not needing approval while demanding ratings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis meltdown. His claim of not needing approval immediately contradicted by his rating demands shows his complete lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: Where am I... in Ohio?!
jokes.setup: Charlie telling a joke about a man with a mule
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical punchline that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Charlie's joke makes no logical sense, which is perfect for his character. The randomness of Ohio as a punchline is classically Charlie.

jokes.text: There's this dago fisherman... Oh, shit! No, no, Charlie... Charlie, we got it, we got... Oh, shit! There goes my cock ring!
jokes.setup: Charlie attempting the inappropriate ethnic joke
jokes.punchline: Frank's cock ring falling off again during the chaos
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to multiple setups. Charlie's inappropriate joke combined with Frank's cock ring creates perfect chaos.

jokes.text: They're all here to get Deed-- the most empowered woman in Philly.
jokes.setup: Dee explaining why men are at the bar
jokes.punchline: Her grandiose self-description as the most empowered woman in Philadelphia
jokes.explanation: Dee's inflated self-image after minimal success. The grandiose title she gives herself shows her narcissism.

jokes.text: Hey, are you the whore who bangs everybody? No, I'm not the whore... I-I'm the empowered woman who gives poor ratings to men.
jokes.setup: Man asking Dee about her reputation
jokes.punchline: Dee's attempt to reframe her behavior as empowerment
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Dee's self-image. Her desperate reframing of promiscuity as empowerment shows her self-deception.

jokes.text: You can't derive your self-worth from the opinions of others. No! No, you get your self-worth from when you convince yourself that your tough and that you're straight.
jokes.setup: Dennis offering wisdom about self-worth
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate contradiction with his own delusions
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. Mac immediately missing Dennis's point and listing his own specific delusions is brilliantly self-unaware.

jokes.text: Or that you are a powerful woman... instead of a dirty, dirty whore... who bangs toad people.
jokes.setup: Dennis continuing his wisdom about delusions
jokes.punchline: His brutal assessment of Dee disguised as philosophical insight
jokes.explanation: Dennis using philosophical wisdom as a vehicle for cruelty toward Dee. The 'toad people' description is perfectly Dennis.

jokes.text: Your true power comes not from outside sources, but from the delusional stories that you all convince yourselves of. And no one, no one can take that away from you.
jokes.setup: Dennis offering life advice to the group
jokes.punchline: His sincere delivery of advice that's actually about embracing delusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the entire show. Dennis's sincere advice about embracing delusion perfectly encapsulates Always Sunny's philosophy.

jokes.text: Goddamn it! You son of a bitch!
jokes.setup: Dennis seeing his zero-star rating
jokes.punchline: His immediate explosion after his philosophical moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending to Dennis's arc. His immediate breakdown after preaching about delusion shows his complete lack of self-awareness.
